denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/07/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102969625          Group #: GRP-1375
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-155396
Claim #: CLM-2026-207876
Date of Service (requested): 01/18/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/20/2026 for Adrenal protocol CT related to Incidental adrenal nodule 2.8 cm (E27.8). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Adrenal protocol CT
Diagnosis (as submitted): Incidental adrenal nodule 2.8 cm (E27.8)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0115 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0115 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Coverage requires a defined course of conservative therapy before Adrenal protocol CT. Documentation does not show the required duration or type of conservative care.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with conservative treatment required. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102969625) and reference UM-2026-155396. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.
Funding: This determination applies under your employer's self-funded group arrangement (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Incidental adrenal nodule 2.8 cm (E27.8). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Adrenal protocol CT recommended as the next appropriate step. Hypertension difficult to control; Hounsfield units indeterminate on prior CT. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that conservative treatment required bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
